message:
feat(stdlib): add StringList sl_init/sl_add/sl_find/sl_free (bd-nsx9r)

NetBSD libutil StringList — a tiny growable Vec<char*> with a
public C-ABI struct. Used by ftpd, kvm tools, mtree, and other
BSD utilities. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi.

* StringList struct exported with NetBSD's documented field
  layout: { char **sl_str; size_t sl_max; size_t sl_cur; }.
* sl_init() — malloc the header + an array of 20 char* slots.
  NULL with ENOMEM on alloc failure.
* sl_add(sl, name) — append `name` (no copy — caller owns the
  string lifetime); doubles the capacity via realloc when full.
  Returns 0 on success, -1 with EINVAL/ENOMEM on failure.
* sl_find(sl, name) — linear scan with byte equality (rejects
  prefix-only matches). Returns the originally stored pointer or
  NULL.
* sl_free(sl, all) — release the StringList. With `all != 0`,
  also free() each stored char* (typical when strings were
  strdup'd into the list).

12 abi integration tests covering: empty list invariants, single
add + pointer-identity check, capacity growth at 64 entries (two
realloc rounds), find hit/miss/empty/NULL-needle/NULL-sl,
prefix-vs-full-match rejection, sl_free(NULL) safety, sl_add(NULL)
EINVAL, sl_free(sl, all=1) double-free safety with libc::strdup'd
strings. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
